title: hieracium sabaudum subsp. vagum
intro: Hieracium sabaudum subsp. vagum, also known as Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, providing several health benefits. Hieracium sabaudum subsp. vagum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in folk medicine and traditional herbal remedies.
